channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Get ready for Google I/O 2026
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is scheduled for May 19-20, with registration now open. The event will focus on updates related to Android, AI, Chrome, and Cloud.

item.title: Turn creative prompts into interactive XR experiences with Gemini
item.description: Google's Android XR team is utilizing Gemini's Canvas feature to simplify the creation of interactive XR experiences, allowing developers to prototype easily on Samsung Galaxy XR headsets.

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google introduced FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model for on-device function calling, enabling efficient AI tasks on mobile devices without internet connectivity.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports various third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect agents to tools like GitHub and Notion for real-world applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will feature a save the date puzzle experience that showcases how AI can enhance event engagement before the main keynotes.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, featuring technical tracks on Gemini and zero-trust security.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: TensorFlow 2.21 has launched LiteRT, a faster framework for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference, supporting PyTorch and JAX.

item.title: SharePoint: Extending AI in SharePoint using custom skills
item.description: SharePoint is extending its AI capabilities with customizable skills that allow businesses to adapt solutions to their unique needs, launching in May 2026.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Improved organization for muted and meeting chats
item.description: Microsoft Teams is introducing improved organization for chats, grouping muted and meeting chats for easier access, with a general availability date in May 2026.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: Following feed in Viva Engage
item.description: Microsoft Viva Engage will offer a new feed type to help users keep up with posts from their networks, featuring updates from leadership and communities, available in June 2026.

item.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Catalog – Migration tool in Unified Catalog to migrate classic glossary terms to Unified Catalog
item.description: Microsoft introduced a migration tool in its Unified Catalog to help users transfer classic glossary terms and their relationships, set for preview in April 2026. This tool aims to streamline data management processes for users.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Facilitator detects and answers questions
item.description: Microsoft Teams has added a feature called Facilitator that detects unanswered questions during meetings and offers to find answers via web search, launching in June 2026. This aims to improve meeting productivity by providing instant information.

item.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
item.description: Anthropic accidentally took down thousands of GitHub repositories in an attempt to protect its leaked source code but later retracted most of the takedown notices. This incident highlights the challenges of managing intellectual property in the tech industry.

item.title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
item.description: Meta plans to power its new Hyperion AI data center in South Dakota with ten natural gas plants, emphasizing its commitment to energy-intensive AI operations. This move reflects the growing energy demands of AI infrastructure.

item.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
item.description: The article titled 'Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier' lacks content for summarization. Further details are needed to provide context and significance.

item.title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
item.description: Cognichip has raised $60 million to develop AI-designed chips, claiming it can significantly reduce development costs and timelines. This innovation could revolutionize the semiconductor industry by making chip production more efficient.

item.title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is set to host leaders from various tech companies on April 30, with limited registration available. This event aims to foster networking and collaboration among industry leaders.

item.title: AI can push your Stream Deck buttons for you
item.description: Elgato's Stream Deck 7.4 update allows users to control their devices using AI assistants, automating button presses through the new Model Context Protocol. This enhances user experience by integrating AI into everyday tasks.

item.title: Falcon Perception
item.description: The article titled 'Falcon Perception' lacks content for summarization. Further details are needed to provide context and significance.

item.title: Baidu’s robotaxis froze in traffic, creating chaos
item.description: Baidu's robotaxis malfunctioned in Wuhan, causing traffic chaos and trapping passengers, with reports of accidents due to the vehicles freezing mid-traffic. This incident raises concerns about the reliability of autonomous vehicle technology.

item.title: Microsoft 365 Copilotに「Copilot Cowork」など追加
item.description: Microsoft has added new features to its 365 Copilot, including 'Copilot Cowork', enhancing collaboration tools within the suite. This update aims to improve productivity and streamline workflows for users.

item.title: Anthropic signs deal with federal government
item.description: Anthropic has signed a deal with the federal government focused on AI safety and tracking economic data. This partnership highlights the government's interest in regulating and understanding AI technologies.

item.title: Anthropic signs deal with federal government
item.description: Anthropic has signed a deal with the federal government focused on AI safety and tracking economic data. This partnership highlights the government's interest in regulating and understanding AI technologies.

item.title: ion-cli added to PyPI
item.description: The ion-cli, a physics simulation runtime for AI agents, has been added to the Python Package Index (PyPI). This addition provides developers with new tools for creating AI simulations.

item.title: lefx 0.3.1
item.description: The LangSmith Evaluation Framework (lefx 0.3.1) has been released, offering a plug-and-play evaluation system for LangChain, LangGraph, and LangSmith projects. This framework simplifies the evaluation process for developers.

item.title: Meta's new structured prompting technique makes LLMs significantly better at code review
item.description: Meta has introduced a new structured prompting technique that significantly improves large language models' (LLMs) performance in code review tasks. This method requires no additional training or tools, making it accessible for immediate use.

item.title: Anthropic Accidentally Exposes Source Code for Claude Code
item.description: Anthropic accidentally exposed source code for its Claude Code AI, attributing the leak to human error while confirming that no customer data was compromised. This incident raises concerns about data security in AI development.

item.title: ccmeter 0.1.8
item.description: The ccmeter 0.1.8 tool has been released, allowing users to measure their actual usage limits of Claude Code. This tool helps users manage their resources more effectively.

item.title: Morning Headlines 4/1/26
item.description: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services is aligning health technology leadership to improve data liquidity and affordability. This initiative aims to enhance healthcare technology integration.

item.title: Nomadic is making video data searchable for AI model training after raising $8.4M in funding
item.description: NomadicML Inc. has raised $8.4 million in seed funding to make video data searchable for AI model training, addressing challenges in video data management for autonomous vehicles. This funding will help advance their technology.

item.title: Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open-source LiteLLM project | TechCrunch
item.description: Mercor, an AI recruiting startup, confirmed it was hit by a cyberattack linked to the open-source LiteLLM project, with an extortion group claiming responsibility. This incident highlights vulnerabilities in AI-related projects.

item.title: Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open source LiteLLM project
item.description: Mercor, an AI recruiting startup, confirmed it was hit by a cyberattack linked to the open-source LiteLLM project, with an extortion group claiming responsibility. This incident highlights vulnerabilities in AI-related projects.

item.title: OpenAI raises $122 billion in boosted funding round
item.description: OpenAI has raised $122 billion in a funding round featuring major investors like Amazon and Microsoft, with a notable $3 billion from individual investors. This funding is crucial as OpenAI prepares for its IPO.

item.title: Anthropic seals new multi-billion dollar deal with Albanese government to track, advance use of AI
item.description: Anthropic has secured a multi-billion dollar deal with the Albanese government to enhance AI usage and tracking in Australia. This partnership aims to advance AI development and regulation.

item.title: This small brand could mess up Apple’s 2027 plans
item.description: A small brand is rumored to disrupt Apple's plans for 2027 with a new product launch early next year. This could impact Apple's market strategy and product lineup.

item.title: PHP AI Models
item.description: The PHP AI Models package encapsulates constants used by AI models, aimed at developers working with PHP 8 and web services. This tool enhances the development of AI applications.

item.title: OpenAI: The Fix Is In
item.description: OpenAI's recent funding announcement signals its aggressive approach towards an IPO, alongside other major AI companies like SpaceX and Anthropic. This reflects the competitive landscape in AI.

item.title: langchain-litellm 0.6.3
item.description: The langchain-litellm 0.6.3 package provides integration between LiteLLM and LangChain, facilitating smoother interactions for developers. This enhances the usability of AI tools.

item.title: CNBC Daily Open: Markets rally as Trump signals Iran war could end soon
item.description: The CNBC Daily Open reports a market rally as Trump suggests a potential end to the Iran war, which could alleviate global supply chain pressures and recession fears in the U.S.

item.title: Toyota's Woven Capital appoints new CIO and COO in push for finding the 'future of mobility' | TechCrunch
item.description: Toyota's Woven Capital has appointed a new CIO and COO to enhance its focus on finding innovations in mobility, cybersecurity, and autonomous driving. This reflects Toyota's commitment to future technologies.

item.title: Meta Deploys AI to Accelerate and Enhance Risk Review During Product Development
item.description: Meta has launched an AI-driven Risk Review program to enhance product safety by identifying potential risks earlier in the development process. This initiative aims to streamline safety assessments and improve overall product quality.

item.title: pytorch-ignite 0.6.0.dev20260401
item.description: PyTorch Ignite 0.6.0.dev20260401 is a lightweight library designed to assist in training neural networks within the PyTorch framework. It aims to simplify the training process for developers.

item.title: pytorch-tokenizers 1.2.0
item.description: PyTorch Tokenizers 1.2.0 is a package offering common tokenization tools in both Python and C++. It provides essential functionalities for natural language processing tasks.

item.title: NeuroView AI Glasses Claims Evaluated: The Smart Wearable Language Translation Eyewear Device Everyone is Raving About
item.description: NeuroView AI Glasses are a smart wearable device that offers real-time language translation and HD video capture. The review covers pricing and key consumer considerations for this innovative technology.

item.title: scrubfile added to PyPI
item.description: The Scrubfile tool has been added to PyPI, allowing users to scrub personally identifiable information (PII) from PDFs, images, and DOCX files with a single command. This tool emphasizes local processing for privacy.

item.title: language-model-common 1.0.24
item.description: Language-model-common 1.0.24 provides a framework to enhance the Langchain library, including new loading configurations. This update aims to improve the usability of language models.

item.title: What is OpenAI’s “social contract” policy push? #lifestyle
item.description: OpenAI is initiating a policy push centered around a 'social contract' concept, signaling a significant shift in its operational framework. This comes amid reports of a large funding announcement and internal discussions.

item.title: Salesforce Just Turned Slack Into an AI Operating System With 30+ New Features
item.description: Salesforce has transformed Slack into an AI operating system, introducing over 30 new features aimed at enhancing productivity and task orchestration, positioning it as a competitor to Microsoft and Google.

item.title: General scales unlock AI evaluation with explanatory and predictive power
item.description: A new automated methodology for evaluating AI systems has been developed, utilizing rubrics to assess cognitive and intellectual demands. This approach aims to enhance understanding of AI capabilities and predict performance.

item.title: Daily Hacker News for 2026-03-31
item.description: The Daily Hacker News for March 31, 2026, highlights the top-rated articles, including discussions on coding fonts and various tech topics. This serves as a snapshot of trending discussions in the tech community.
